description_long: This object consists of a rectangular solar panel mounted on a metal frame, positioned at an angle to maximize sunlight exposure. The solar panel is connected via a visible black cable to a compact, box-shaped orange and white unit, which likely houses the electrical components such as batteries and an inverter. The orange unit features ventilation grilles, indicator lights, and multiple ports for electrical connections, suggesting it is designed for off-grid or portable solar power generation. The solar panel has a blue surface with a grid of white lines, typical of photovoltaic cells, and is supported by a sturdy, ground-mounted frame. The orange box is equipped with small wheels for mobility and has branding on one side. The overall design is compact and suitable for urban environments, such as parks, sidewalks, or temporary installations.
description: A compact solar energy system with a blue photovoltaic panel on a metal frame, connected to an orange and white control unit with wheels and visible cabling.
description_view_0: Side view showing the angled blue solar panel and the orange control unit directly behind it.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the full face of the solar panel and the side of the orange unit with ports and vents.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the orange box with branding and a partial view of the solar panel behind it.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite direction, showing the thin profile of the solar panel and the back of the orange unit with visible cabling.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_cabling_visibility: black cable connecting the solar panel to the orange unit is visible along the ground
attribute_array_configuration: single solar panel in a standalone configuration
